title: Status Lines, Informational Responses, Content Rules, and Response Parsing · Runtime Contract: Head Response Metadata
summary: A client parser accepts malformed status codes, stops at 100 Continue, assigns content to HEAD and 204, treats successful CONNECT as ordinary HTTP, and retries after an incomplete response. Planned artifact: Add and verify the head response metadata increment in response control-data and content decision parser. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse HEAD response metadata as if GET were selected while never consuming response content bytes.
Process zero or more permitted informational responses before the final response with bounded count and protocol-specific handling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Head Response Metadata · Theory Head Response Metadata
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 3022 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound informational responses and transition HEAD, no-content, and CONNECT responses correctly; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then inject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and close before any downstream forwarding. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse HEAD response metadata as if GET were selected while never consuming response content bytes.
